summary: Found 3 critical issue(s) and 2 warning(s) across 864 scenarios. Performance degradation detected in 17 area(s). Results assessed relative to: 500 concurrent users, handling mixed data types, burst/peak usage patterns, deep analysis.
plain_summary:
We found some problems that could affect your FastAPI API service (Mycode Ai) under real-world conditions.

- During running calculations across components, memory grew from 0MB to 14MB. This is moderate and unlikely to cause problems on a typical server.
- During running calculations across components, memory grew from 0MB to 7MB. This is moderate and unlikely to cause problems on a typical server.
- During running calculations across components, memory grew from 0MB to 3MB. This is moderate and unlikely to cause problems on a typical server.
- During loading the /api/preflight endpoint, 50 runtime errors occurred. This means your app may crash or produce wrong results under this load.
data_type_note: You indicated mixed data types — myCode ran all scenario categories at equal priority. Format-specific stress testing (malformed inputs, encoding edge cases) is planned for v2.

findings.title: Endpoint /api/preflight degrades under concurrent load
findings.description: This issue appears at 50 items. At your current 25 users with small datasets (~50 items each), your total data volume is approximately 1,250 items — already past this threshold. This is affecting your application now. Your /api/preflight endpoint becomes unresponsive at 50 concurrent connections. This is at or below your expected 500 concurrent users — your users will experience failures. Results showed significant variance between test rounds — infrastructure conditions may affect these measurements.
findings.diagnosis: Your FastAPI endpoint delegates blocking work to the default thread pool. At 50 concurrent requests, the pool saturates and requests queue — new requests wait for a thread, causing cascading timeouts.
findings.prompt:
[CRITICAL] Endpoint /api/preflight degrades under concurrent load
Location: src/mycode/web/app.py
Deps: fastapi, uvicorn
Failed at load level: 50
Diagnosis: Your FastAPI endpoint delegates blocking work to the default thread pool. At 50 concurrent requests, the pool saturates and requests queue — new requests wait for a thread, causing cascading timeouts.
Fix: Create a dedicated ThreadPoolExecutor sized for your expected concurrency, or convert blocking operations to native async (async def + await).
See attached JSON for full diagnostic data.

grouped_findings.title: Memory baseline limits concurrent capacity
grouped_findings.description: Your application uses 56MB per process. On a 2GB server, this supports approximately 27 concurrent sessions — well below your expected 500 users. You will need to optimize memory usage or scale your infrastructure.
grouped_findings.diagnosis: Your application uses 56MB per process. This is a baseline issue, not a memory leak — memory stays flat under load.
grouped_findings.prompt:
[CRITICAL] Memory baseline limits concurrent capacity
Location: src/mycode/web/app.py
Deps: fastapi, uvicorn
Diagnosis: Your application uses 56MB per process. This is a baseline issue, not a memory leak — memory stays flat under load.
Fix: Use lazy imports for heavy modules (import inside functions, not at module level), defer loading large models/data until first request, or increase server memory.
See attached JSON for full diagnostic data.

findings.title: Computation Coupling (Mycode constraints depth to timeout)
findings.diagnosis: Your application's processing time grows with input size. At large inputs, this becomes the bottleneck.
findings.prompt:
[WARNING] Computation Coupling (Mycode constraints depth to timeout)
Diagnosis: Your application's processing time grows with input size. At large inputs, this becomes the bottleneck.
Fix: Use chunked or streaming processing instead of loading all data into memory, add pagination for large result sets, and consider caching intermediate results for repeated queries.
See attached JSON for full diagnostic data.
